title: 'Talk without condition such as resignation': Govt to oppn leaders over NEET debate
content: The government is engaging opposition leaders to resolve the Parliament deadlock over the NEET paper leak.  Discussions aim to end disruptions which have stalled proceedings since the Monsoon session began.  The Centre is ready for debate without preconditions, including minister's resignation.  A meeting of floor leaders is likely soon to build consensus for the discussion.  This outreach follows repeated adjournments due to protests on examination irregularities.

title: Amazon cuts jobs in team focused on tech that Google, Microsoft says is their ‘biggest’ goals
content: Amazon has reduced roles within its Artificial General Intelligence unit.  This restructuring impacts teams focused on model customization and data services.  The company continues significant investment in artificial intelligence development and infrastructure.  These job cuts follow recent leadership changes within Amazon's AI division.  Amazon aims to sharpen its focus on key customer-centric AI initiatives.

title: Consumer watchdog fines Chaayos Rs 50k for auto-adding service charge in bills
content: The Central Consumer Protection Authority fined Chaayos fifty thousand rupees for automatically adding service charges.  This consumer watchdog ruled that restaurants cannot force customers to pay voluntary tips.  Chaayos must refund the collected service charge to the complainant.  The company also needs to remove default service charges from all its billing systems.  This order reinforces that service charges are optional and not mandatory.

title: Who is Ashok Sharma? Farmer's son makes India debut after 154.2 kmph IPL breakthrough
content: Ashok Sharma debuted for India in the T20I against Zimbabwe in Harare. He earned his cap after a strong domestic and IPL performance. His journey began in a small village, supported by family sacrifice. Sharma impressed in the Syed Mushtaq Ali Trophy and IPL 2026. He is now part of India's balanced bowling attack.

title: One monsoon, many fault lines: How heavy rains exposed Pune’s infrastructure gaps
content: Pune experienced record monsoon rainfall, overwhelming its infrastructure and preparedness.  Heavy rains caused widespread waterlogging and transport disruptions across the city.  Newly built infrastructure faced scrutiny after facing its first monsoon test.  A garbage mound collapse tragically claimed lives, highlighting planning failures.  The events revealed systemic weaknesses in urban planning and disaster management.

title: Canada college to protesting Indian students: Seek legal advice, retain imp documents
content: Indian students protest after Portage College's business management diploma graduates faced work visa rejections.  The Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada stated their programs were not eligible for permits.  Portage College encourages affected students to retain important documents and seek legal advice.  The college cannot overturn decisions made by the immigration authority.  Affected students should consult official government materials and obtain independent advice.

title: Bangladesh President likely to resign soon: Reports
content: Bangladesh President Mohammad Shahabuddin is expected to resign soon, according to political sources.  This comes amid government discomfort and speculation about his continuation in office.  Reports suggest the government is unhappy over alleged attempts to contact ousted Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina.  Hasina, sentenced to death in absentia, plans to return to Bangladesh in December.  Parliamentary Speaker Hafizuddin Ahmed will serve as interim president if Shahabuddin resigns.

title: Why scientists are dropping fish from airplanes into mountain lakes
content: Utah's Division of Wildlife Resources stocks remote mountain lakes by dropping fish from airplanes. This method efficiently reaches inaccessible waters, maintaining fish populations for anglers. Tiny trout and other species are released, with survival rates exceeding ninety-five percent. Biologists carefully select species, often using sterile fish to control numbers and protect native ecosystems. This practice balances recreation with conservation in challenging mountainous terrain.

title: Ships trapped near Strait of Hormuz could become nurseries for invasive species
content: Ships lingering in the Persian Gulf could unintentionally facilitate the spread of harmful marine invaders worldwide. Extended anchorage allows marine organisms to thrive on the hulls, creating significant biofouling concerns that threaten global maritime commerce. These invasive species can inflict extensive ecological and economic harm on coastal areas. It is imperative for nations to join forces to address biofouling issues and prevent future outbreaks of invasion.

title: Uber lays off 10% of support staff, ends remote work, and blames AI for the first time
content: Uber has laid off 10% of its customer support staff and told remote workers on the team to relocate to hub offices. It's the company's second round of job cuts in eight weeks, following a 23% reduction in its people division in June. For the first time, Uber has named AI as a reason—after burning its entire 2026 Claude Code budget in four months.

title: DDA launches housing scheme with 25% discount: 1,200+ flats, starting from Rs 33 lakh
content: The Delhi Development Authority has launched a new housing scheme for government employees and professionals. This scheme offers over twelve hundred ready-to-move flats with a significant twenty-five percent discount. Registration for these affordable homes opens on July twenty-fourth and booking begins August fifteenth. Flats are located in Narela and will be allotted on a first come, first served basis.

title: Indian judoka Arun Kumar withdrawn from 2026 CWG after failing dope test
content: India's Commonwealth Games campaign suffered a blow as judoka Arun Kumar was ruled ineligible after an adverse finding in an out-of-competition NADA dope test. The promising 73kg judoka, a gold medallist at the 2025 Taipei Asian Open, was set for his CWG debut. His withdrawal reduces India's Glasgow 2026 contingent from 125 to 124 athletes.

title: Quote of the day by Chadwick Boseman
content: In his poignant reflections, Chadwick Boseman illustrates the transformational power of confronting adversity. He recalls a significant setback: being dismissed for advocating genuine depictions in acting, which imparted critical lessons on his internal struggle and artistic perspective. Originally from South Carolina and a Howard University graduate, Boseman rose to fame, bravely battling cancer until his untimely passing.

title: Beyond Hormuz: Gulf is spending billions to find new routes to global markets
content: Gulf nations are accelerating pipeline projects to bypass the Strait of Hormuz.  These new routes aim to move oil without relying on the narrow waterway.  Several major pipeline projects are under construction or being planned.  Diversification efforts aim to protect regional oil exports from disruptions.  These alternative routes face their own risks and increased costs.

title: Gujarat battered by monsoon fury as floods inundate south, rail services hit
content: Heavy rainfall continues to batter Gujarat and Maharashtra, disrupting train services significantly. Rivers have overflowed their banks, causing widespread inundation in towns and villages. Multiple rescue operations are underway as floodwaters enter residential areas. Authorities have issued a red alert, forecasting extremely heavy rainfall for the region. Residents in vulnerable areas are urged to remain indoors and avoid travel.

title: Iran claims strikes destroyed Patriot systems, THAAD radar at US bases in Jordan, Kuwait
content: In a bold assertion, Iran's IRGC declared the destruction of Patriot systems and THAAD radar deployed in Jordan. Following recent tragic losses of US service members in the region, drone strikes launched at US bases in Kuwait inflicted damage on crucial depots and fuel tanks. The Iranian Army further revealed conducting drone strikes on three Kuwaiti military installations, though these claims from the US military remain unverified.

title: France's deadly June heatwave caused nearly 5,800 excess deaths, highest toll since 2003
content: France recorded over five thousand excess deaths during a recent heatwave.  This extreme weather event caused the highest death toll since two thousand three.  Elderly individuals accounted for two-thirds of the total fatalities recorded.  Hospitals faced significant pressure from a surge in heat-related illnesses.  Scientists link these intense heatwaves to human-induced climate change.

title: India's first CWG 2026 medal is already assured before the opening ceremony - Here's how
content: India has secured its first medal at the Commonwealth Games 2026 before the opening ceremony, with Olympic medallist Lovlina Borgohain assured of at least bronze after receiving a semifinal bye. She faces T.K.B.P. Taafaki on July 31 for a place in the final. India begins its Glasgow campaign with a 124-member contingent targeting success across multiple sports.

title: Anna Hazare begins maun andolan in support of protesting students
content: Anna Hazare started a silent protest in Ahilyanagar supporting Delhi students.  Students are protesting alleged examination irregularities and paper leaks.  The Cockroach Janta Party also demands accountability and the minister's resignation.  Clashes occurred between demonstrators and police during a march in Delhi.  Sonam Wangchuk remains hospitalized after a prolonged hunger strike.

title: 'Will book you in drug case': Mumbai cop threatens student protesters on cam; inquiry ordered
content: Mumbai Police ordered an inquiry into a viral video showing a police driver threatening students. The driver allegedly warned of fabricated criminal cases and drug planting charges. This incident occurred while Mumbai witnessed protests supporting a New Delhi student agitation. The driver has been removed from his current posting pending the inquiry's completion. Police are verifying facts to address the serious allegations raised by the video.

title: IIT Delhi students built Rs 565cr dairy startup, revolutionising India’s cattle market
content: Three former semiconductor engineers—Sujoy Chakravarty, Ravi Mehta and Biman Chattopadhyay—built a successful chip IP company, sold it to Synopsys, and launched Bengaluru-based Quanfluence to develop room-temperature quantum computers. After months of studying quantum technologies, they chose photonics as the most practical approach. Supported by academic experts, the startup now offers commercial cloud-based quantum computing solutions, demonstrating India's growing capabilities in frontier technology while highlighting the importance of lifelong learning, engineering excellence and innovation.

title: Woman stands in front of Mumbai Police vehicle during CJP protest, video goes viral
content: A young woman has become a symbol of protests against the alleged NEET paper leak. Rhiya Ahir confronted police in Mumbai, questioning their response to student demonstrations. The Cockroach Janta Party's protests are expanding across cities following earlier clashes in Delhi. Police have registered multiple cases and booked hundreds for unlawful assembly and violating orders. Protesters demand the Union education minister's resignation or direct engagement with students.

title: ‘You take credit for Mars, now take responsibility': Priyanka targets PM Modi over protests
content: Priyanka Gandhi Vadra criticized Prime Minister Narendra Modi regarding student protests and police actions. She questioned the government's handling of demonstrations and alleged police brutality against students. The Prime Minister announced fast-track courts for paper leak cases amid ongoing parliamentary protests. Opposition MPs demanded the Education Minister's resignation while BJP leaders called for debate. Tensions briefly escalated between rival party members outside Parliament.

title: India’s parking crisis is turning into a major civic headache
content: India faces a severe parking shortage due to rapid motorisation and poor urban planning.  Vehicle numbers have doubled in a decade, overwhelming city infrastructure.  Historic areas and newer suburbs struggle with limited space for growing car ownership.  Informal parking thrives while multi-level facilities remain underutilized and enforcement is challenging.  Technology and better public transit are key to managing this growing urban problem.

title: UP housing board to demolish 44 illegal properties; traders express concern for livelihood loss
content: UP housing board will demolish 44 sealed shops in Meerut's Shastri Nagar area. This action follows a Supreme Court order regarding illegal constructions and commercial activities. Property owners have fifteen days to comply with demolition notices. Many traders face livelihood crises due to the impending demolition of their businesses. Officials are working to identify and address all non-compliant properties across the city.

title: 10 biggest numbers from Google quarter that has made 'history'
content: Alphabet reported a strong second quarter with $119.8 billion in revenue. Google Cloud saw an impressive 82% year-over-year revenue jump. Gemini app reached 950 million active users, and Search AI Mode crossed one billion. YouTube advertising revenue increased by 13% to over $11 billion. Google's AI models are processing 22 billion tokens every minute.

title: 'Dhamaal 4' BO Day 14 [LIVE]: Eyes Rs 185 crore worldwide finish before third weekend
content: 'Dhamaal 4', starring Ajay Devgn, Arshad Warsi, Riteish Deshmukh and Jaaved Jaaferi, is tracking Day 14 of its theatrical run with the India net inching past Rs 135 crore. The Indra Kumar-directed 'Dhamaal 4' is now deep into its second week at the box office, with weekday numbers having cooled off considerably since the film's franchise-best third day and with Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey continuing to hold a chunk of screens, all eyes are now on how the film performs in its upcoming third weekend.

title: Mercedes-AMG E53 Hybrid launched at Rs 1.45cr: Up to 100 km on battery
content: Other equipment includes a Burmester sound system, head-up display, wireless phone charger, fingerprint sensor, heated front sports seats, ambient lighting with logo projection, automatic climate control and rear door sunblinds. Safety features include a 360-degree camera, tyre pressure monitoring system and a driver monitoring camera.

title: Bamboo fans, shared shade and pure grit keep Jantar Mantar alive amid CJP protest
content: Volunteers are providing essential support at the CJP protest. They offer shade and water to demonstrators enduring the heat. Many individuals stepped in without formal assignments to help. Young and old alike demonstrate unwavering resolve and solidarity. This organic network ensures the protest continues despite challenges.

title: 'Hamesha der kar deta hoon...': Tharoor quotes Niazi to target PM's paper leak response
content: Shashi Tharoor used a poetic couplet to criticize the government's paper leak response. Prime Minister Modi announced fast-track courts for swift punishment for offenders. This action plan aims to safeguard the future and welfare of the nation's youth. The Congress party has described the Centre's response as significantly delayed. Protests continue at Jantar Mantar and a hunger strike enters its twenty-sixth day.

title: 'Bite incidents every day': Delhi HC takes up stray dog issue, launches suo motu PIL
content: The Delhi High Court has started a suo motu public interest litigation to monitor stray dog management.  Notices were issued to the Delhi government and Municipal Corporation of Delhi.  Authorities must submit a status report detailing steps taken by July thirty-first.  The court expressed concern over the increasing stray dog population in the city.  This action follows Supreme Court directions for all High Courts to oversee compliance.

title: 'Name is Nothing': CJP on PM Modi's 'nothing more important than youth' post
content: Prime Minister Modi announced fast-track courts for NEET paper leak cases. Abhijeet Dipke of Cockroach Janta Party criticized the government's approach. He satirically highlighted the demand for Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. Protesters continue demonstrations seeking Pradhan's removal and compensation for affected students. The group insists the minister's resignation remains a non-negotiable demand.

title: Once played by Vallabhbhai Patel, bridge seeks India's 'chess' moment: 17-yo Anshul leads its revival
content: Once enjoyed by Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, bridge has faded from India's sporting landscape. Seventeen-year-old prodigy Anshul Bhatt hopes to change that. Inspired by Viswanathan Anand's belief that bridge is where chess was two decades ago, Anshul is winning world titles, mentoring young players, conducting research, and building a movement to give the mind sport its long-awaited breakthrough in India.

title: PV Sindhu's China Open campaign ends in heartbreak
content: PV Sindhu's China Open campaign ended in the second round after a spirited 21-16, 20-22, 18-21 defeat to world No. 4 Chen Yufei. Fresh from her Japan Open title, Sindhu squandered four match points in the second game before the Tokyo Olympic champion staged a comeback to win a gripping 90-minute contest and reach the quarterfinals.

title: Simone Biles reveals her biggest fear and how Jonathan Owens helped her
content: Simone Biles has inspired millions with her Olympic success, but her journey was shaped by childhood hardship and a lasting fear of financial insecurity. This story explores how those early experiences influenced her relationship with money, the struggles she overcame before becoming a gymnastics legend, and how NFL star Jonathan Owens became a constant source of love and support. From foster care to Olympic glory, Biles’ story is not just about winning gold medals but also about finding stability, trust, and happiness beyond the sport.

title: Historic relocation of 30 beluga whales begins from closed Canada's Marine park
content: In a significant conservation effort, six beluga whales have been relocated from Canada's Marineland to various aquariums in the United States. This operation, which involved careful planning and the use of specialized transport containers, saw four whales arriving at the Shedd Aquarium while two found a new home at SeaWorld San Antonio.

title: Booked 2 plots, got no agreement: Delhi consumer panel orders builder to refund Rs 19.73 lakh
content: A consumer commission ordered a developer to refund nearly twenty lakh rupees.  This decision followed a housing project delay and the absence of a signed agreement.  The Indian Air Force officer had booked two plots and paid the full amount.  He approached the commission after the developer refused a refund or alternative project.  The commission ruled that without a binding contract, a refund was the appropriate resolution.

title: 'Might hinder ongoing trial': SC cancels Sonam Raghuvanshi's bail in honeymoon murder case

title: Govt.'s resolve to ensure bright future for youth: Amit Shah on fast-track courts for paper ‘leak’ cases
content: The Union Home Minister called this an “important step” taken by PM Modi against those who toy with the future of students

title: ‘Will plant drugs’: Mumbai cop threatens protesting students, police order probe
content: The policeman seen in the video worked at Sion Police Station till he was moved out, the State’s Home Department said

title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: Centre invites protesters for talks, CJP declines
content: CJP says the talks must either happen at Jantar Mantar or at a neutral venue; spokesperson Ashutosh Ranka reacted to PM Modi’s announcement of fast-track courts, saying that the real issue to be addressed is why paper leaks happen

title: Houses of two terrorists ‘blasted’ after Anantnag attack; J&K CM says mass arrests will not restore peace
content: Locals allege the security forces used explosives to demolish the houses after asking the inmates to leave the structures; the inmates refused to comment on the demolitions; locals said a mosque and a vehicle also suffered damages in these demolitions

title: ‘Jana Nayagan’ release: ‘Thalapathy’ Vijay pays tribute to MGR
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay’s Jana Nayagan has finally hit theatres after months of delays. The film pays tribute to former Chief Minister and screen legend MGR with AI-generated voice recreations, iconic song references and political symbolism. Vijay is the second sitting Tamil Nadu Chief Minister after MGR to have a film release while in office

title: Defunct street lights along Chettuva bridge in Thrissur, Kerala, raise safety concerns
content: Residents have been urging the authorities to make the street lights functional and carry out permanent repairs to the bridge’s damaged surface. Accidents have become common on the damaged bridge

title: Centre invites protesters for talks at J.P. Nadda’s office; CJP rejects offer
content: Addressing the students gathered at Jantar Mantar, spokesperson Ashutosh Ranka said the CJP said talks would only be held at Jantar Mantar or neutral venue

title: ‘Jana Nayagan’ director H Vinoth interview: On Thalapathy Vijay’s support during controversies
content: Filmmaker H Vinoth speaks about his upcoming film ‘Jana Nayagan,’ starring Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay, the unwavering support he received from the former star during the turbulent months prior to its release, what success means for a politically-charged film that unfortunately leaked online, and more

title: CJP protest: Police stop students in autorickshaws from reaching Jantar Mantar
content: Students say they were not being allowed to go to the protest site; police say students are not allowed because ‘Section 144’ (Section 163 of the BNSS) is in effect

title: Kerala Opposition Leader Pinarayi Vijayan targets PM Modi over NEET irregularities; hits out at Centre for police crackdown on student protests in New Delhi
content: Students’ Federation of India takes out a ‘Charter of Rights’  march to Kerala Lok Bhavan in solidarity with the youth protest spearheaded by the Cockroach Janata Party in New Delhi

title: Telangana CM questions BRS’s silence on demand for Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation
content: Revanth refers to Intermediate examination paper evaluation controversy during the previous BRS regime, defends his governments initiatives and argues another dam would have to be constructed near Medigadda if water has to be stored

title: Amaravati emerging as liveable city, education hub, say Ministers after review
content: P. Narayana, Gottipati Ravi Kumar and Dola Bala Veeranjaneya Swamy reviewed the progress of roads, government buildings, iconic towers, residential quarters for gazetted and non-gazetted officers, and accommodation for MLAs and MLCs

title: U.S.-based software engineer arrested in Chennai over hoax bomb threats to persons, institutions across India
content: Sarath Subramanian Shankar, a native of Ambasamudram in Tirunelveli district, who was residing in the U.S. is suspected to have sent a series of hoax bomb threat emails between 2023 and 2025 to airports, educational institutions, public officials and other high-profile targets across India.

title: Final report on Sabarimala gold ‘theft’ will be filed without delay, SIT tells Kerala HC
content: ADGP’s report says final opinion of the lab provides an explanation of the process allegedly adopted by the accused in committing the offence

title: Sabarimala gold theft case: SIT detains former TDB president P.S. Prasanth for questioning
content: Prasanth’s detention comes nearly three weeks after SIT questioned Unnikrishnan Potti, the prime accused in the case. The move comes hours after Prasanth posted on Facebook that his arrest in the case was imminent

title: PMK urges Centre to replace Cauvery Management Authority with empowered board
content: Alleging Karnataka’s reservoirs held sufficient water to release Tamil Nadu’s share, Dr. Anbumani said the CWMA lacked the authority to enforce its decisions or act against violations

title: Himachal farmers pioneer sustainable cultivation of endangered Himalayan herb ‘Kutki’
content: A stolon-based harvesting method developed by researchers is helping conserve the IUCN-listed medicinal herb while creating a new source of income for farmers in Himachal Pradesh

title: Social activist Anna Hazare undertakes 'maun andolan' to support protesting students
content: “Anna began his protest at 11 a.m. and ended it at 1 p.m.,” the social activist’s aide Datta Awari told PTI

title: Namakkal man arrested for posting ‘defamatory’ remarks against T.N. CM Vijay
content: The police said Thiagu was arrested after he allegedly shared defamatory content targeting the Chief Minister through his social media account

title: Parliament Monsoon Session Day 4 highlights: Both Houses adjourned for the day without any transaction of business amid Oppn. protests
content: Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju accused the Opposition of evading NEET debate and insisted on government participation; Kharge reiterated demand of Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation

title: School student seriously injured in wild gaur attack in Kerala’s Idukki
content: Boy was waiting for an autorickshaw to take him to school when a wild gaur suddenly emerged and attacked him

title: At least one killed in Tapi district as 1,200 others rescued from rain-hit areas in Gujarat
content: PM Modi assures all necessary assistance to Gujarat government;Umbergaon taluka received over 1,100 mm rains in just 16 hours

title: ‘Virtual magnet’ claims reveal why EVs need their rare-earth magnets
content: A large amount of effort has gone towards replacing permanent magnets in the rotor with either electromagnets or just a piece of iron. However, efficiency, starting torque capability, and the maximum speed achievable are some important constraints that have kept these motors out of EVs

title: What caused the NHPC tunnel disaster in Sikkim’s Namchi? | Explained
content: Methane trapped inside rock is suspected to have triggered the fatal blast. But how does this happen?

title: While India added teachers, Telangana lost nearly 7,400 of them in a decade amid rising student enrolments
content: The drop in number of teachers has been accompanied by a sharp drop in number of colleges, rise in Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER), decline in pupil-teacher ratio, and rise in number of student enrolments

title: ‘Jana Nayagan’: a timeline on the twisting path to theatrical release
content: Chief Minister Vijay’s last movie as star actor - Jana Nayagan - dropped at theatres across the world after protracted censoring issues, and leaking of high-definition movie footage. Here is a timeline of events.

title: T.N. Minister holds meeting with stakeholders ahead of ‘presence-less’ registration launch
content: In the first phase of “presence-less” registration, the first sale of plots and flats will begin on August 17

title: The rise of the Cockroaches: Timeline
content: From a controversial courtroom remark to a nationwide agitation, here is how the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) movement unfolded

title: Karnataka Chief Minister urges enterprises to direct CSR spending towards primary education
content: State government would constitute a dedicated team to monitor CSR projects across Karnataka, says D.K.  Shivakumar

title: BDA puts checks on builders seeking plan modifications in Bengaluru
content: The BDA has made it clear that no modified building plan can be sanctioned once the permissible Floor Area Ratio has been fully utilised and an Occupancy Certificate issued for the entire project

title: Kerala professor’s arrest in BDS student’s death raises larger questions on safeguards against ‘illegal’ arrests
content: Violations of mandatory provisions and fundamental rights prescribed by the Constitution in the case came to light on an interaction of the sessions judge with the accused

title: NHAI revises toll charges on Bengaluru-Nelamangala and Bengaluru-Kolar highway stretches
content: According to separate public notices issued by the Project Implementation Unit (PIU), Bengaluru, the revised toll rates will be applicable at the Bengaluru-Nelamangala toll plazas on NH-48 and the Hedaginabele, Agrahara, Krishnarajapura and Sundarapalya toll plazas on the Bengaluru-Bethamangala section of NH-75

title: What is the Sabarimala gold ‘theft’ case? | Explained
content: The detention of a former TDB president has brought the Sabarimala gold ‘theft’ case back into focus. Here’s a look at the allegations, the investigation and what lies ahead

title: Sikkim tunnel disaster: 22 bodies recovered; search and rescue ops continue for remaining victims
content: Officials said personnel of multiple agencies, including those specialising in coal-bearing areas, conducted operations until midnight on Wednesday under difficult condition

title: In Pictures: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay's 'Jana Nayagan' opens to fan frenzy
content: Fans across Tamil Nadu celebrated the release of Chief Minister Vijay’s 'Jana Nayagan' with grand FDFS festivities, marking what is being billed as the star-actor’s final film

title: Supreme Court may step in if closure of metro stations persists, says CJI
content: Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant informed the Bar that he had passed an administrative order directing that no adverse orders be passed solely on account of the non-appearance of lawyers

title: Bengaluru voices at Freedom Park: ‘Jaldi resign kar Dharmendra Pradhan, Sarjapur jaana hai’
content: Scores of students gathered at Freedom Park in Central Bengaluru condemning the police lathi charge on students who were marching to Parliament protesting against NEET paper leak and irregularities

title: What is Rule 267 in Rajya Sabha?
content: The caveat in the rules is that a suspension can only occur for a matter already listed in the day’s business, meaning that the matter of discussion(business) should be listed in the topics for discussion in the Rajya Sabha for that day.

title: Mask up if you have a heart condition: IMA expert, as Andhra Pradesh COVID-19 cases reach 32
content: Rajeev Jayadevan of the National IMA COVID Task Force said even a fever strains the heart, and that people with heart disease and other conditions should wear masks in crowded places

title: Meghalaya honeymoon murder: SC sets aside Sonam Raghuvanshi’s bail, gives her three weeks to surrender
content: The court directed Sonam Raghuvanshi to surrender within three weeks; the Bench said that Ms. Raghuvanshi’s continued liberty at this stage would “hinder” the ongoing trial

title: One public toilet for up to 30,000 persons in Bengaluru: Survey report
content: According to B.PAC, the city’s public sanitation infrastructure has not kept pace with its population growth, now estimated at over 1.4 crore

title: Six government health facilities in rural Karnataka equipped with solar power systems to ensure uninterrupted service
content: They have been equipped with energy-efficient medical devices, including portable baby warmers, phototherapy units for newborns, foetal monitors, assisted ventilation systems, and vaccine carriers with temperature control

title: Who is Dharmendra Pradhan? The man at the centre of the CJP education protests
content: Thousands of protesters from the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) took to the streets demanding education reforms and the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. But who is the man at the centre of the controversy?

title: Man dies days after arrest in Thoothukudi; family alleges custodial torture
content: Arunachalam was arrested for illegal sale of liquor bottles; he was released on July 17, after which his family admitted him to Thoothukudi government hospital, where he was being treated in the neurosurgery ward

title: Why over 250 landless beneficiaries of Kerala’s Arippa land struggle are trapped in bureaucratic deadlock
content: Distribution of title deeds in February 2026 has stalled due to a conflict between Revenue and Forest Departments

title: Pedestrian opens latch, detained students escape from police van in Mumbai
content: A video went viral where a man approaches a police van carrying the detained protesters and opens the latch, enabling several students to jump out and run away foiling police attempt to shift student protesters in Mumbai’s Chembur area

title: Kerala actor assault case: HC declines to suspend sentence of two more convicts
content: Division Bench holds that there is sufficient evidence on record to prove the alleged conspiracy and that they had 'executed their plan with precision’

title: Gitanjali Angmo says students’ image being tarnished in media
content: Gitanjali Angmo said those who knew the protesting students had witnessed their extraordinary patience, discipline and restraint

title: FIR against Instagram user for allegedly abusing Prime Minister Narendra Modi over student protest in Delhi
content: The FIR was registered based on a complaint by Abhilash Reddy M (38), president of the BJP unit in Sarvagnanagar constituency

title: Tear gas in India: The law, the science and the human cost
content: As CJP protests reignite debate over ‘Tear Gas’, here’s how tear gas works, the laws governing its use, why it can turn fatal, and what to do if exposed

title: India slams Pakistan at UN over cross-border terrorism; says Kashmir integral, inalienable part
content: At the High-Level Open Debate of the UN Security Council on July 22, India was compelled to reply after Islamabad ‘misused’ the discussion to push a ‘false narrative’ said India's Permanent Representative to the United Nations, Ambassador Parvathaneni Harish

title: Sabarimala gold theft case: SIT arrests former TDB president P.S. Prasanth
content: Former Travancore Devaswom Board member A. Ajikumar too arrested. Both being interrogated at Kollam Police Club

title: CBI says no evidence found against kingpin Sanjeev Mukhiya in NEET-UG 2024 paper leak case
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. title: Parliament Session 2026 Live Updates: Opposition MPs Protest At Parliament Over NEET Leak, Demand Dharmendra Pradhan's Resignation
